steps.title: Spot the defining phrase
steps.content: ”On the basis of their instrumental value for promoting youth interests” is the signal. Instrumental value means practical benefits or outcomes, not principles. This tells us Bidadanure is not claiming youth underrepresentation is inherently unfair (that's the consensus view). Instead, she's arguing based on concrete practical effects—how quotas would actually benefit young people.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice C is correct: it acknowledges youth underrepresentation may be fair in principle but argues quotas would produce better outcomes for young people—exactly an instrumental argument. The others either deny the passage's fairness consensus (B and D) or contradict its reasoning about experience (A). That matches choice C — C is correct.
